inline_comments.message:
If this is going to be used only in this deployment, then it should not be defined in the global section of vfc/values.yaml, instead it should be defined in vfc-ems-driver/values.yaml in the config section.
MSB was required in all the deployments, thats why it was kept in the vfc/values.yaml global section to allow all the pods to use this value.

This should be internalPort in case of NodePort.
In this case they are both the same though and ClusterIP is used.

This should be internalPort in case of NodePort.
In this case they are both the same though and ClusterIP is used.

You do not technically need to name the container ports.  I think you can remove this.

If you want to use portNames there is an example here:

oom/kubernetes/helm/starters/onap-app/templates/service.yaml

However, I tried something in my environment and the error goes away if you use lower case i.e.

  portName: hw-http
  port2Name: hw-https
